steps.title: Recognize the noun needing modification
steps.content: The blank comes after “nonpresidents” in the phrase “one of many nonpresidents ______ on US currency.” A noun in this position needs a verb form to describe what the noun is or does. The structure suggests a reduced relative clause—shortened from “nonpresidents who appear.”

steps.title: Apply the infinitive form
steps.content:
The infinitive to appear completes the standard pattern “one of many [noun] to [verb],” giving us “one of many nonpresidents to appear on US currency”—establishing Morris as one of many nonpresidents whose image appears on currency.

The finite verb forms (appears, did appear, had appeared) don't fit this construction and create grammatically incorrect structures.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
